puzzle.title: The Call for a Heavenly Feast
puzzle.intro: A group of devoted followers stood by their prophet, their faith tested by a profound request. They sought a sign not from doubt, but to strengthen their hearts, asking for a miracle that would descend from the heavens and serve as a testament for all generations. Who were these companions?
clues: We were the close companions and helpers of a prophet sent to the Children of Israel.
We famously pledged our allegiance, declaring, 'We are the supporters of Allah'.
Our prophet was given the ability to perform great miracles, like healing the blind and lepers, and giving life to the dead by Allah's permission.
Despite our strong belief, we made a bold request for a tangible sign to further reassure our hearts.
We specifically asked our prophet to pray for a table laden with food to be sent down to us from the heavens.
Our request was met with a divine promise, but also a stern warning: if any of us disbelieved after this sign, a punishment would come like no other.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The Companions of the Cave (Ashab al-Kahf)
The People of the Ditch (Ashab al-Ukhdud)
The Disciples of 'Isa (Al-Hawariyyun)
The Ansar of Madinah
The Companions of Musa

trial.label: What Was Our Request?
options: To see Allah manifestly
A table spread with food from heaven
For a she-camel to emerge from a mountain
To be granted eternal life in this world
For a river to gush forth from a rock
trial.answer: A table spread with food from heaven

location.label: What Was the Stated Purpose?
options: To hoard provisions for themselves
To challenge the prophet's authority
To prove their superiority over other nations
To reassure their hearts and be witnesses
To gain worldly power and kingship
location.answer: To reassure their hearts and be witnesses

outcome.label: What Was the Divine Response?
options: The request was outright denied
An immediate punishment of a storm
A promise to send it with a stern warning
They were turned into apes and swine
A spring of water was provided instead
outcome.answer: A promise to send it with a stern warning

puzzle.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوٓا۟ أَنصَارَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ مَنْ أَنصَارِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَـَٔامَنَت طَّآئِفَةٌۭ مِّنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَكَفَرَت طَّآئِفَةٌۭ ۖ فَأَيَّدْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ عَلَىٰ عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ ظَـٰهِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: O you who have believed, be supporters of Allāh, as when Jesus, the son of Mary, said to the disciples, "Who are my supporters for Allāh?" The disciples said, "We are supporters of Allāh." And a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved. So We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant. (61:14)
